_comment: Gold-set template — the canonical format read by src/pipeline/calibration.py (load_gold_set). Replace the skeleton items with ~20-40 REAL answers from a proxy run, hand-labeled by a human (never by a model — independence is the point). Run: python -m src.pipeline.calibration
_labeling_rules: present: true/false — is the brand mentioned at all in the answer?
prominence: one of recommended_first | mid_pack | buried | also_ran | absent. Relative within the answer (who is named first vs. buried). absent if and only if present=false.
framing: one of positive | neutral | negative. NO 'n/a' — absent brands get 'neutral' (matches the judge's default).
labels: include one entry per brand for the client AND every competitor listed on the item, even when absent.
fact_sheet: inline text (paste the relevant facts) or null. Only items WITH a fact sheet count toward flag-detection agreement.

expect_accuracy_flags: boolean — should the judge flag at least one wrong claim about the CLIENT in this answer? (v1 calibration is binary: flagged-when-expected. Note specific expected flags in _notes for future type-level calibration.)
Freeze the file once judge testing starts; keep a few of the clearest items aside as judge-prompt examples and never calibrate on those.

items._notes: Skeleton item 1 — replace every field. Example shape: client mentioned but with a wrong claim -> expect_accuracy_flags true.
items.answer: {paste the FULL raw engine answer here}

items._notes: Skeleton item 2 — a 'losing query': client absent, no fact sheet (accuracy not assessed for this item).
items.answer: {paste the FULL raw engine answer here}
